children.text: Allocation among trusts; basis of allocation
The Alien Property Custodian shall allocate among the various trusts the funds in the “unallocated interest fund” (as defined in

children.tail: ). Such allocation shall be based upon the earnings (determined by the Secretary of the Treasury) on the total amounts deposited under

children.text: Payment to persons entitled to distributions

The Alien Property Custodian, when the allocation has been made, is authorized and directed to pay to each person entitled, in accordance with a final decision of a court of the United States or of the District of Columbia, or of an opinion of the Attorney General, to the distribution of any portion of such unallocated interest fund, the amount allocated to his trust, except as provided in subsection (c) of this section.
Credit against amount invested in certificates
In the case of persons entitled, under paragraph (12), (13), (14), or (16) of subsection (b) of

children.tail: , to such return, and in the case of persons who would be entitled to such return thereunder if all such money or property had not been returned under paragraph (9) or (10) of such subsection, and in the case of persons entitled to such return under subsection (n) of
, an amount equal to the aggregate amount allocated to their trusts shall be credited against the sum of $25,000,000 invested in participating certificates under paragraph (1) of subsection (b) of

. If the aggregate amount so allocated is in excess of $25,000,000, an amount equal to the excess shall be invested in the same manner. Upon the repayment of any of the amounts so invested, under the provisions of section 4 of the Settlement of War Claims Act of 1928, the amount so repaid shall be distributed pro rata among such persons, notwithstanding any receipts or releases given by them.

children.text: The unallocated interest fund shall be available for carrying out the provisions of this section, including the expenses of making the allocation.
Section 4 of the Settlement of War Claims Act of 1928, referred to in subsec. (c), is section 4 of

children.tail: , which is not classified to the Code.

children.text: Section was formerly classified to section 26 of the former Appendix to this title prior to editorial reclassification and renumbering as this section.

children.tail: , struck out “average rate of” before “earnings” in second sentence.

children.text: Functions vested by law in Alien Property Custodian and Office of Alien Property Custodian transferred to Attorney General by Reorg. Plan No. 1 of 1947, § 101, eff.

children.tail: , set out in the Appendix to Title 5, Government Organization and Employees.
